message:
Update meta-index for Tantivy 0.22+ API compatibility

Applies the same Tantivy 0.22+ migration as content-index (d72fb43),
updating meta-index to use IndexSettings, TantivyDocument, and simplified
reload policy configuration.

Changes
-------
**meta-index (ultrasearch/crates/meta-index/src/lib.rs)**

+ Import additions
  ```rust
  use tantivy::{
      Index, IndexSettings,                       // NEW: IndexSettings
      IndexWriter,
      schema::document::TantivyDocument,          // NEW: TantivyDocument
      schema::*
  };
  ```

~ open_or_create_index() - add IndexSettings parameter
  ```rust
  pub fn open_or_create_index(path: &Path) -> Result<MetaIndex> {
      let (schema, fields) = build_schema();
      let settings = IndexSettings::default();    // NEW
      let index = if path.join("meta.json").exists() {
          Index::open_in_dir(path)?
      } else {
          Index::create_in_dir(path, schema, settings)?  // NEW parameter
      };
      Ok(MetaIndex { index, fields })
  }
  ```

~ open_reader() - use default reload policy
  OLD:
  ```rust
  let reader = meta
      .index
      .reader_builder()
      .reload_policy(tantivy::ReloadPolicy::OnCommit)
      .try_into()?;
  ```

  NEW:
  ```rust
  let reader = meta.index.reader_builder().try_into()?;
  ```

  Rationale:
  - Default reload policy is now ReloadPolicy::OnCommitWithDelay
  - Explicit OnCommit is rarely needed (only for instant-visibility use cases)
  - Metadata index updates are batched by scheduler, so delay is acceptable

~ to_document() - TantivyDocument type
  OLD signature: `fn to_document(...) -> tantivy::Document`
  NEW signature: `fn to_document(...) -> TantivyDocument`

  OLD construction: `tantivy::Document::new()`
  NEW construction: `TantivyDocument::default()`

Consistency with content-index
-------------------------------
This commit mirrors the Tantivy migration in content-index (commit d72fb43)
to maintain API consistency across both index subsystems:

| Aspect              | meta-index | content-index |
|---------------------|------------|---------------|
| IndexSettings       | ✓          | ✓             |
| TantivyDocument     | ✓          | ✓             |
| Reload policy       | default    | OnCommitWithDelay |
| Merge policy        | default    | default       |

The only difference: content-index explicitly specifies
ReloadPolicy::OnCommitWithDelay, while meta-index uses the builder default
(which is the same value). Both approaches are equivalent.

Performance characteristics
----------------------------
- IndexSettings::default() uses log merge policy (write-optimized)
- Default reload policy (~1s delay) reduces reader churn
- Metadata index size is typically smaller than content index
  (filenames vs. full text), so merge overhead is less critical

Related
-------
- §c00.4.1 meta-index design (filename + metadata fields)
- commit d72fb43: content-index Tantivy 0.22+ migration
- Cargo.toml: tantivy = "*" (tracks latest via wildcard policy)
- AGENTS.md: nightly + latest versions mandate

Testing
-------
Existing tests continue to pass:
- schema_builder_has_expected_fields()
- bincode_serialization_roundtrip()

Integration tests pending (c00.9.x milestone).
